verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 is primarily ER-resident but documented to reach the plasma membrane and extracellular environment via two complementary mechanisms: (1) secretory pathway trafficking and (2) direct insertion into lipid bilayers through high-affinity binding to negatively charged phospholipids (PS, cardiolipin). Surface HSPA5 is functionally active as a viral receptor and signal-transducing co-receptor, and stress-inducible translocation to the PM is well-established in cancer and ER-stressed cells. The protein reaches the outer face via its own lipid-binding domains, not merely as transient cargo. Lacks transmembrane domain but compensates via oligomeric phospholipid insertion.
